headline: US-Israeli strikes kill Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei and collapse Strait of Hormuz traffic by 70%, triggering the most consequential week for global markets since Russia's invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.

top_stories.title: Operation Epic Fury: US-Israeli Strikes Decapitate Iranian Regime, Strait of Hormuz Collapses
top_stories.summary: On Feb 28, the US and Israel launched joint Operation Epic Fury, striking 1,000+ targets across 24 Iranian provinces including IRGC facilities, air defenses, missile sites, and nuclear infrastructure. Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed when his official residence was destroyed. Iran retaliated with strikes on 27 US bases and Gulf Arab states, including missile hits on Dubai and Abu Dhabi. The Strait of Hormuz experienced a 70% traffic collapse after the IRGC broadcast closure warnings and attacked 3 commercial ships.
top_stories.investor_impact: Oil surged 13% with Brent at $72.48 and forecasts of $100/bbl if conflict persists. Gold spiked past $5,300 on safe-haven flows. Gulf state sovereign bonds face repricing. Energy-import-dependent economies (JPN, KOR, DEU, TWN) face inflation and growth headwinds. US recession probability jumped from 25-30% to 40-50%. Dow futures fell 517 points. Defense contractors (Lockheed, Raytheon, Northrop) are direct beneficiaries.

top_stories.title: Pakistan Declares Open War on Afghanistan, Bombing Kabul and Kandahar
top_stories.summary: Pakistan declared 'open war' on Afghanistan on Feb 27, launching Operation Ghazab Lil Haq with airstrikes targeting TTP and ISIS-K positions in Kabul and Kandahar. Pakistan rejected all calls for dialogue from Iran, the EU, Qatar, and China. Cross-border trade has been suspended and refugee flows are disrupting border provinces. This pits a nuclear-armed state against Taliban-governed Afghanistan in the most severe escalation since the Taliban takeover.
top_stories.investor_impact: Pakistan sovereign bonds (CCC+/Caa1) face further spread widening with political risk premium at 1,650 bps. PKR under renewed depreciation pressure. Defense spending surge will strain Pakistan's $18.4B FX reserves and complicate IMF program compliance. China's CPEC investments face elevated operational risk. Cross-border trade suspension disrupts Central-South Asian supply chains.

top_stories.title: US Trade Policy Constitutional Crisis: SCOTUS Strikes Down IEEPA Tariffs, Section 122 Imposed
top_stories.summary: The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 on Feb 20 that the President lacks tariff authority under IEEPA, striking down the existing tariff framework. Trump immediately pivoted to Section 122 of the Trade Act, imposing a 15% global tariff. Additional tariffs of 20% cumulative on China and 25% on Canada and Mexico take effect March 4. The legal challenge raises questions about $200B in already-collected tariff revenue and creates a fundamental restructuring of US trade law.
top_stories.investor_impact: Trade-exposed manufacturers face acute uncertainty. Canada (75%+ of exports to US) and Mexico (USMCA renegotiation in 2026) are most vulnerable. S&P 500 posted its largest monthly decline in a year. The $200B tariff refund question creates contingent liability for US fiscal position. Safe-haven rotation into Treasuries pushed US 10Y below 4% for the first time in 4 months.

top_stories.title: Nuclear Arms Control Framework Collapses Across Three Fronts
top_stories.summary: The global nuclear order deteriorated simultaneously on three fronts this week. The New START treaty -- the last nuclear arms control agreement between the US and Russia -- expired without extension. North Korea conducted hypersonic missile tests on Jan 4 (Hwasong-16B solid-fueled IRBM) and convened a rare 9th Party Congress. Iran's nuclear infrastructure was directly targeted in the US-Israeli strikes. Russia is planning new ICBM tests. The global nuclear framework is in its most precarious state since the Cold War.
top_stories.investor_impact: Record global defense spending of $2.72T (SIPRI). Defense equities globally benefit from sustained rearmament cycle. Japan's $58B defense budget, Germany's EUR 88.5B, and Poland's $38B reflect the new paradigm. US defense spending at $997B. Risk premiums across conflict-proximate nations widening. Nuclear risk hedging strategies warranted for institutional portfolios.

top_stories.title: EU-Mercosur Agreement and Ukraine Gains Provide Counterbalancing Positive Signals
top_stories.summary: Amid the dominant negative developments, two positive stories emerged. The EU-Mercosur Partnership Agreement was signed Jan 17 and entered provisional application -- the largest FTA ever negotiated, covering 780 million people. Argentina ratified it 69-3 in the Senate and passed landmark labor reform. Separately, Ukraine's southern counteroffensive recaptured 300+ sq km in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, while Flamingo cruise missiles struck Russia's Votkinsk ICBM factory. The IMF approved an $8.1B loan anchoring a $136.5B support package.
top_stories.investor_impact: EU-Mercosur benefits European automakers (reduced tariffs) and South American agricultural exporters. Argentina sovereign bonds may see modest compression on legislative victories. Ukraine sovereign bonds remain distressed but the combination of IMF support, battlefield gains, and peace talk momentum provides tail-risk upside. European defense equities continue to benefit from sustained conflict.

regional_summaries.North America: The US faces converging crises: SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs prompting a pivot to Section 122 (15% global tariff), while Operation Epic Fury against Iran killed 3 US soldiers and drove recession probability to 40-50%. Canada and Mexico face 25% tariffs effective March 4, with Canada's deep US trade integration (75%+ of exports) making it uniquely vulnerable. Canada-US relationship health shows strong_decrease. Mexico's GDP trend is decrease (1%) and judicial reform with only 13% voter turnout undermines rule of law.
regional_summaries.Europe: Germany launched a historic EUR 500B infrastructure fund and record EUR 88.5B defense spending while facing energy import pressure from the oil surge. The UK underwent a major cabinet reshuffle after Deputy PM Rayner's resignation. Denmark called snap elections for March 24 over the Trump-Greenland standoff. ECB held at 2% for a fifth consecutive meeting; oil-driven inflation complicates the rate path. EU-Mercosur entered provisional application despite France's opposition. Ukraine's counteroffensive recaptured 300+ sq km, and IMF approved $8.1B in new lending. Poland's defense spending reached $38B amid PM-President institutional gridlock. Georgia's political crisis entered its second year with EU accession suspended.
regional_summaries.East Asia: China faces GDP deceleration (4.8%) amid US tariff escalation to 20% cumulative and conducted unprecedented Taiwan blockade simulations. NPC opens March 5 to set economic direction; Trump-Xi summit planned for March 30. Japan's LDP won a supermajority enabling constitutional revision with defense spending at $58B. South Korea's Yoon was sentenced to life for insurrection; KOSPI hit a fresh record high despite political turbulence. Taiwan faces acute vulnerability with 5% energy independence, 69% chokepoint exposure, and PLA drone incursion over Pratas Island. North Korea tested hypersonic missiles in January.
regional_summaries.South Asia: Pakistan's declaration of open war on Afghanistan dominates the region. Sovereign credit at CCC+/Caa1 with war expenditure threatening $18.4B FX reserves. India faces 50% US tariffs over Russian crude purchases but a new US-India trade deal may offset impact; GDP growth at 6.6% but decelerating. Bangladesh held its first post-uprising election on Feb 12 with BNP winning a landslide (209 seats). Sri Lanka remains in selective default with 100.8% debt-to-GDP but GDP has recovered to 5%.
regional_summaries.Middle East & North Africa: The region faces its most severe crisis since the 2003 Iraq War. Iran's regime is in existential crisis following Khamenei's killing; 42.4% inflation approaching hyperinflation. The Strait of Hormuz 70% traffic collapse critically affects UAE (76% chokepoint exposure), Qatar (66%), Kuwait (64%), Oman (62%), Saudi Arabia (52%), and Iraq (51%). Israel participated in strikes but faces Iranian retaliation, a March 31 budget deadline that could dissolve the government, and Moody's downgrade to Baa1. Saudi Arabia took a neutral stance and signed a $9B US arms deal. Turkey faces regional security risk on its eastern border with 34.9% inflation. Egypt's Suez Canal revenues threatened by resumed Houthi Red Sea attacks. Yemen Houthis announced resumption of commercial shipping attacks.
regional_summaries.Sub-Saharan Africa: Ethiopia has the highest risk premium in the dataset at 2,700 bps, with selective default credit status and Tigray conflict resumption threatening the 2022 peace agreement. Nigeria suffered a Boko Haram massacre killing 170+ in Kwara state amid 23% inflation and labor protests. Ghana and Mozambique remain in selective default territory. DR Congo launched an offensive against Rwandan-backed M23 rebels, affecting cobalt and coltan supply chains. Sudan's civil war continues as one of the world's worst humanitarian crises. South Africa's JSE benefited from improved investor sentiment with ZAR strengthening.
regional_summaries.Latin America: Argentina delivered a productive legislative week: the Senate passed Milei's labor reform 42-28 and ratified EU-Mercosur 69-3, though inflation remains at 41.3%. Brazil benefits from EU-Mercosur as the largest Mercosur economy, with the Bovespa gaining 16.3% in February and BRL appreciating 2.1%. Mexico faces 25% US tariffs effective March 4 with GDP at 1% and judicial reform undermining investor confidence. Peru installed its 9th president in a decade after the Chifagate scandal with elections scheduled April 12. Colombia elections on March 8. Venezuela remains in turmoil after US capture of Maduro in January.
regional_summaries.Central Asia: Kazakhstan's economy remains stable with 6.1% GDP growth, though oil export revenues face Hormuz disruption risk given Central Asian pipeline routing through Russia. Uzbekistan continues steady growth. The broader region faces indirect effects from the Pakistan-Afghanistan war, with potential refugee spillovers and trade disruption along traditional routes. Russia's economic stress (CBR cut to 15.5%, 9% inflation) ripples through the region's remittance-dependent economies.
regional_summaries.Southeast Asia: Singapore's 94% chokepoint exposure -- the highest in the dataset -- creates acute vulnerability to the dual Hormuz-Red Sea disruption. Indonesia held rates at 4.75% for a fifth consecutive meeting amid rupiah weakness from capital outflows. Vietnam holds legislative elections on March 15 with 70% chokepoint exposure. Philippines faces a governance crisis as the Marcos corruption scandal forced the House Speaker and Senate President to step down. Thailand and Malaysia remain relatively insulated but face energy cost pressures.
regional_summaries.Oceania: Australia is a relative safe haven with Aaa/AAA credit rating and commodity export benefits from higher oil and mineral prices. The RBA hiked rates to 3.85% -- its first hike since November 2023 -- on higher-than-expected inflation. ASX 200 posted its strongest month since 2019. New Zealand remains stable with minimal direct exposure to the Middle East conflict. Both economies face indirect effects through Asian trade linkages and oil import costs.

market_context.summary: Markets experienced a historic risk-off rotation driven by Operation Epic Fury and the Hormuz crisis. Gold surged past $5,300 intraday to $5,278 at close, oil spiked 13% on disruption fears, and US Treasuries rallied with 10Y yields falling below 4% for the first time in 4 months. The DXY index at 97.96 reflects safe-haven dollar demand. Equity markets were mixed with the S&P 500 posting its largest monthly decline in a year (-0.43% weekly), while KOSPI hit a record high and Bovespa surged 16.3% monthly.

notable_moves.driver: Safe-haven demand from US-Iran military conflict; surged past $5,300 intraday
Iran conflict, Strait of Hormuz 70% traffic collapse; forecasts of $100/bbl if sustained
Flight-to-quality; fell below 4% for first time in 4 months; strongest monthly decline in a year (-25bps in Feb)
Safe-haven spillover from gold; correcting from all-time high of $121.67 in January
Safe-haven flows despite broader dollar softness against EUR and JPY
BRL appreciated to strongest since May 2024 on EM revaluation; EU-Mercosur optimism
Supply disruption fears from Middle East conflict; advanced ~45 cents in February
EU-Mercosur optimism; strong 16.3% monthly gain; BRL appreciation
Iran conflict, recession fears (40-50% probability), tariff uncertainty; Dow futures -517 points
Controlled devaluation by CBRT; 20.68% monthly depreciation; record low for lira

data_quality_summary.notes: First pipeline run. 997 official stats records, 82 financial data records, 63 news events, 35 military/conflict records, 31 trade/sanctions records, 43 political/regulatory records. Two anomalies flagged: Libya 15.6% GDP growth (plausible post-conflict recovery), Strait of Hormuz -70% traffic (confirmed). Zero rejections. Data quality assessment: HIGH.
